QF.id: IV Fixed Income Instruments and Bond Markets_23

question.text: A callable bond gives the right to:

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** A callable bond gives the ISSUER the right to redeem the bond before its maturity date. The issuer exercises this right typically when market interest rates fall below the bond's coupon rate, allowing refinancing at a lower cost.
**The Trap:** A puttable bond gives the bondholder the right to redeem early. A bond with both call and put options gives rights to both parties.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Embedded options in bonds are tested in Series IV and affect bond valuation (option-adjusted spread). The concept also applies to callable corporate bonds in Series V-A.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Callable bonds trade at lower prices (higher yields) than comparable non-callable bonds because the call feature benefits the issuer at the expense of the investor.
